findings.claim: The record's Status line and title claim a graph-gateway design, yet §3.4.3 fixes a concrete confidence-derivation function — Environment base 0.9 × exp(−Δt/τ), τ = 90 days, Catalog/Standards/Governance base 1.0 — as 'the implemented behavior, executed identically on every capture.' The body simultaneously declares this derivation's authority routed to the forthcoming inherited-confidence-derivation decision record. A concrete function form and constants committed as executed behavior is a decision the record makes; routing its authority elsewhere while shipping it as implemented is a claim-fidelity strain — the record decides more than its stated scope (execution mechanics) by fixing a derivation the substrate (DDR-002 §4) explicitly does not fix a concrete function for.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.4.3 (derivation fixed as implemented behavior) vs SDD-001 §2.2 / §9 (derivation authority routed to forthcoming decision record); DDR-002 §4 'DDR-002 fixes that the rollup exists ... not a concrete function'

findings.claim: The v0.3.0 change-log row lists revision carriers 'run-010-sdd-001/audit.md + run-011-sdd-001/audit.md' but the §9 Cross-References deliberation-artifact entry for v0.3.0 additionally names 'run-011-sdd-001/disposition.md' as a ratifying carrier. The two enumerations of what the v0.3.0 amendment rests on do not match; a reader cannot tell whether the disposition record is a ratifying source of the amendment or not.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §10 v0.3.0 row (two carriers) vs SDD-001 §9 Cross-References v0.3.0 revision carriers (three carriers incl. disposition.md)

findings.claim: The record states its retraction timing 'stands' and that #25's ratified biconditional 'is unsatisfied during a retraction proposal's pre-decision window,' then declares the scoping clause that would satisfy it to be 'proposed additive DDR-002 amendment content' with 'the harness implements from ratified text, before and after amendment.' The record thus decides to ship a design it concedes is non-conformant with the ratified invariant #25 as currently worded, resting the reconciliation on an amendment that does not yet exist. Whether this is an accepted consequence declared as such, or a smuggled retraction of #25 conformance, is the claim-fidelity question: the consequence (a live window where a ratified biconditional fails) is surfaced, but the record's own summary voice ('This SDD's timing stands') asserts resolution the cited substrate does not support.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.5.4 ('#25's biconditional as ratified is unsatisfied during a retraction proposal's pre-decision window' + 'This SDD's timing stands') vs DDR-002 §7 #25 (ratified biconditional, no pre-decision scoping clause)

findings.claim: Attacked the scope boundary for smuggled decisions: whether the record quietly assigns authorship the substrate leaves unassigned. Checked §3.4.6 (advance-artifact-lifecycle), §2.2 (Attestation/Actor/Role, PromotionDecision transport, rollup function, zero-evidence posture), and #3/#5/#12-verification against ADR-002 §2.6's author/executor/authorizer principle. Each unassigned authority is routed by name with rejection-until-assigned semantics ('unassigned authority is unexecutable, not defaulted'), and no operation defaults its author to the gateway. The record decides only execution mechanics for these, holding the authorship decisions to their owning records. The boundary held.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.6 (author/executor/authorizer separation; gateway never the authoring authority); DDR-002 §5 (Solution lifecycle-transition authority routed to SDDs)

findings.claim: Attacked the §3.3.8 determination for a smuggled schema decision: the record declares 'no CI invariant of its own' for the provenance-survival retrieval affordance, which could be the gateway SDD deciding a matter reserved to DDR-002. Checked against the substrate — DDR-002 §5/§7 #20 already guarantees frozen-layer reachability, existence, completeness, and at-promotion binding, and DDR-002's Named Gaps explicitly reserve 'whether the affordance warrants its own CI invariant' as an open question routed to the knowledge-service SDD. The record answers exactly the question routed to it and explicitly defers the Named-Gap text pruning to a DDR-002 amendment ('not performed here'). The record decides what it is authorized to decide, no more. The boundary held.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §7 #20 + Named Gaps 'Provenance-survival retrieval index/affordance ... whether it warrants its own CI invariant ... → knowledge-service SDD'

findings.locus: §3.4.3 capture-evidence — inherited-confidence derivation, branch (ii) Environment class
findings.claim: The Environment-class inheritance formula `0.9 × exp(−Δt/τ)` computes Δt from the cited node's `observed_at`, but the design does not specify the reference time against which Δt is measured (capture time? request time? a fixed epoch?). DDR-002 §4's canonical confidence definition specifies 'source authority × freshness ... decayed by `observed_at`' without fixing the anchor either, so this SDD introduces an operative constant (Δt) with no defined second operand — the derivation as written cannot be executed deterministically as claimed ('executed identically on every capture').

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.4.3 — Δt defined only from observed_at, reference-time operand unspecified; contradicts the same section's 'executed identically on every capture' determinism claim

findings.claim: §3.2 lists `CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE` as an enforced-invariant error type and §3.4.3 states the only reachable underivable case is an Extension node carrying neither node `confidence` nor `observed_at`. But branch (ii)'s non-Extension totality rests on class base × freshness for every non-node-confidence plane; a Catalog/Standards/Governance node cited without a resolvable freshness input is not addressed — the claim that the derivation is 'total by construction' is asserted, not substantiated for the base-1.0 branches, where no freshness operand is consumed at all yet the branch still claims a derived value. Whether these branches are total is not demonstrated in the record.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.4.3 — 'derivation is total by construction' claimed but the base-1.0 (Catalog/Standards/Governance) branch's totality is unsubstantiated against the single named underivable case

findings.locus: §9 Cross-References — Change Log v0.3.0 revision-carrier list
findings.claim: §9's v0.3.0 revision carriers list `run-010`, `run-011`, and `run-011-.../disposition.md`, while the §10 Change Log v0.3.0 row cites only `run-010` + `run-011` audits and the union disposition. The §9 deliberation-artifact list adds a `disposition.md` carrier not named in the Change Log's own carrier parenthetical; the two carrier enumerations do not resolve to the same set.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §9 vs §10 v0.3.0 row — divergent revision-carrier enumerations for the same version

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record-SKILL — 'Placement, naming, metadata' / 'Status and version' (Change Log is the authoritative carrier of revision history); the finding is a same-document internal-coherence defect between §9 and §10's enumeration of the same v0.3.0 revision carriers

findings.claim: Attacked the write-authority surface for a path by which the gateway could become an authoring authority in violation of ADR-002 §2.6 (gateway is sole executor, never author). Species-2 request schemas carry no author field (authorship-by-gateway unexpressible), and §3.4.6 rejects any Solution lifecycle transition whose author is unassigned (`AUTHOR_VIOLATION`) rather than defaulting it to the gateway. Every mediated write traces to a Species-1 component author or a Species-2 governing decision; no default-to-gateway path was found. The surface held against ADR-002 §2.6 and §6 check 7.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.6 / §6 check 7 — gateway sole executor, never authoring authority; no operation's author defaults to the gateway

findings.claim: Attacked the retraction timing for a conformance gap: DDR-002 #25's ratified biconditional (proposal_kind:retraction ⇔ RETRACTS edge) would be unsatisfied during a retraction proposal's pre-decision window if the RETRACTS edge is written at proposal. The SDD writes RETRACTS at materialization (grounded in DDR-002 §5's terminal-status execution gloss), and explicitly names that the ratified biconditional as-written is unsatisfied in the pre-decision window, implements the harness from ratified text before/after the proposed scoping-clause amendment, and does not silently rely on unratified text. The conformance seam is disclosed, not concealed, and the timing coheres with DDR-002 §5.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §5 / §7 #25 — RETRACTS written at terminal-status materialization; biconditional residue disclosed and harness-bound to ratified text
